_description: SwornAgent project architecture policy. The binary owns protocol, orchestration, state, and control-plane contracts; adapters must not recreate them.

canonical_docs._description: Canonical code and decision records consulted by planning and architecture review.

rules.description: Production files changed by a slice must be declared in planned_files
rules.note: Add the file to the slice plan or record and approve a scoped design allowlist entry.

rules.description: MCP and TUI adapters must submit use cases rather than write authoritative state
rules.note: Route the mutation through the canonical control or state-transition service.

rules.description: Control adapters must call typed services rather than construct Sworn subprocess commands
rules.note: Use the shared command/control application service and return its typed outcome.

rules.description: Orchestration must not discard terminal state or Git persistence errors
rules.note: Return the persistence failure through the typed terminal-outcome boundary.

rules.description: Production code must not add process-global environment or working-directory mutation
rules.note: Pass configuration and repository paths explicitly instead of mutating process state.

rules.description: New orchestration subprocesses must consume cancellation and deadlines
rules.note: Use CommandContext with the owning operation context and tested child cleanup.

rules.description: Agentic child processes must not inherit the complete parent environment
rules.note: Construct an explicit per-driver environment and pass through secrets only by documented opt-in.

rules.description: Provider credentials use canonical ecosystem environment names only
rules.note: Resolve provider keys through the canonical credentials authority.

rules.description: Account and model packages must not reconstruct the shared credential filename
rules.note: Use internal/credentials for path resolution and field-preserving persistence.

rules.description: Orchestration and control surfaces must not hardcode provider/model routing choices
rules.note: Declare models in provider/role configuration and resolve through the canonical registry.

rules.description: Operator guidance must use registered current Sworn commands
rules.note: Render next actions from the canonical command/control registry.

rules.description: Remote notification payloads must not include machine-local worktree paths
rules.note: Send stable project/release/slice identifiers and a canonical remote action instead.

rules.description: Notification transports must not use the process default unbounded HTTP client
rules.note: Use a notifier-owned client with explicit request and total delivery deadlines.

rules.description: New orchestration code must project events through the outbox instead of calling notifier adapters directly
rules.note: Enqueue an operation event for the canonical notification outbox dispatcher.

rules.description: Large single-slice file growth requires an explicit cohesion review
rules.note: Prefer typed seams and cohesive files; explain intentional large analyzers in the design record.
